flow_overview: An e-commerce gift-card flow on a retail storefront that lets shoppers discover gift-card options, configure a UA eGift Card, and prepare it for purchase.
capability_summary: Gift-card discovery from the main storefront
Separate physical and digital gift-card options
Add-to-bag purchase initiation for configured gift cards
Visible gift-card terms and fulfillment information

requirements.text: The system shall allow shoppers to navigate from the main storefront into a dedicated gift-card area.
requirements.rationale: The flow starts on the general retail homepage and then shows a dedicated UA Gift Cards page, indicating supported navigation from the storefront into the gift-card experience.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a gift-card section that supports both gift-card purchasing and gift-card balance inquiry.
requirements.rationale: The gift-card landing page presents purchasable gift-card options and a separate balance-check form, showing both capabilities in one area.

requirements.text: The system shall distinguish between physical gift cards and eGift cards as separate gift-card purchase modalities.
requirements.rationale: The landing page shows UA Gift Cards and UA eGift Cards as different options, and the next screen opens a specific eGift Card product page.

requirements.text: The system shall support navigation from a gift-card discovery page to a detailed purchase page for a selected eGift card product.
requirements.rationale: The sequence moves from a broader gift-card page to a product-detail screen titled UA eGift Card, which is a clear list-to-detail transition.

requirements.text: The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.
requirements.rationale: Across the ordered screenshots, the eGift card form is progressively filled with recipient name, recipient email, sender email, amount, delivery date, and message.

requirements.text: The system shall support choosing an eGift card amount within a visible allowed denomination range.
requirements.rationale: The product page displays a $10.00 to $500.00 range and later shows a $100 amount entry, but screenshots alone do not prove full range enforcement.

requirements.text: The system shall support scheduling eGift card delivery for a selected date rather than only immediate sending.
requirements.rationale: A delivery-date field is present throughout the configuration flow and the page text mentions scheduling a custom delivery date, though actual fulfillment timing is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall allow a shopper to include a personalized message with the eGift card.
requirements.rationale: The message field is visible on the product page and later contains the entered text "gift card," directly showing support for personalized messaging.

requirements.text: The system shall provide an Add to Bag action for the configured eGift card on the product-detail page.
requirements.rationale: A prominent Add to Bag button remains available throughout the configuration flow, indicating that the product can be submitted to the shopping bag from this page.

requirements.text: The system shall preserve previously entered gift-card configuration data while the shopper continues completing later fields.
requirements.rationale: Each successive screenshot retains earlier inputs while new information is added, showing visible state carry-over and form consistency.

requirements.text: The system shall present key gift-card fulfillment and usage information, including delivery timing, redemption channels, fees or expiration status, and discount restrictions.
requirements.rationale: The product page includes descriptive text and bullet points about email delivery timing, redemption locations, no fees or expiration dates, and ineligible discounts, though screenshots do not verify the backend truth of those claims.

requirements.text: The system shall present the selected gift-card product with clear identifying and pricing information on its purchase page.
requirements.rationale: The product-detail page visibly shows the product name, an image of the card, and the price range beside the purchase form, supporting transparent product identification.

requirements.text: The system shall allow customers to check the balance of an existing gift card by entering the card number and PIN.
requirements.rationale: The gift-card landing page includes a Check Your Balance section with fields for card number and PIN and a View Balance action.

requirements.text: The system shall keep the standard shopping-site shell, including category navigation and bag access, available throughout the gift-card flow.
requirements.rationale: The same retail header and bag/navigation controls remain visible on the homepage, gift-card landing page, and eGift card detail screens, indicating a consistent shopping shell.

requirements.text: The system shall add the configured eGift card to the shopper's ongoing cart or bag session after confirmation so it can continue into downstream checkout.
requirements.rationale: The visible flow ends with a completed purchase form and an Add to Bag action, which strongly implies downstream bag state, but the actual bag update is not shown.

requirements.text: The system shall validate that required eGift card purchase information is present before accepting the item for purchase.
requirements.rationale: The page is structured as a multi-field purchase form leading to Add to Bag, so required-field validation is a disciplined inference, even though no explicit error or success state is shown.

requirements.text: The system shall retain previously entered eGift card configuration details while the shopper continues through the configuration flow and adjusts later inputs before submission.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: persistence_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-05
Source gold text: The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.
Generation rationale: The accepted flow already shows progressive population of the eGift form across several screens. Extending that into persistence of earlier entries during continued configuration is a realistic nearby requirement that may be only partly supported by the visible sequence.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.

requirements.text: The system shall provide the purchaser with a confirmation outside the product page flow that reflects the configured eGift card recipient, amount, and scheduled delivery details after the gift card is accepted for purchase progression.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: partially_fulfilled
Mutation family: external_effect_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-15
Source gold text: The system shall add the configured eGift card to the shopper's ongoing cart or bag session after confirmation so it can continue into downstream checkout.
Generation rationale: A downstream confirmation is a plausible extension of adding a configured eGift card into purchase flow. It stays close to the same feature space but depends on effects beyond the visible screenshots, which helps diversify later review outcomes.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall add the configured eGift card to the shopper's ongoing cart or bag session after confirmation so it can continue into downstream checkout.

requirements.text: The system shall restrict scheduled eGift card delivery to valid future dates and provide user-facing feedback when an unavailable date is chosen.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: hidden_state_extension
Source gold requirement: REQ-07
Source gold text: The system shall support scheduling eGift card delivery for a selected date rather than only immediate sending.
Generation rationale: The gold requirement establishes date scheduling as part of the eGift workflow. Adding future-date validity and unavailable-date rejection is realistic, but the actual policy and rule enforcement are not settled by the provided flow alone.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support scheduling eGift card delivery for a selected date rather than only immediate sending.

requirements.text: The system shall make all supported eGift card denominations visibly available within the purchase flow rather than exposing only a partial subset of purchasable amounts.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: abstain
Mutation family: completeness_or_universal_quantifier
Source gold requirement: REQ-06
Source gold text: The system shall support choosing an eGift card amount within a visible and fixed allowed denomination range.
Generation rationale: This strengthens the visible denomination-range idea into a completeness claim about all supported amounts. That is a plausible human-written requirement in the same purchase feature space, but full coverage is difficult to verify from one observed configuration path.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall support choosing an eGift card amount within a visible and fixed allowed denomination range.

requirements.text: The system shall provide a pre-submission review state for the configured eGift card that summarizes recipient details, sender details, amount, delivery date, and message, with an option to edit before finalizing the purchase action.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: missing_visible_step
Source gold requirement: REQ-05
Source gold text: The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.
Generation rationale: A review-and-edit step is a strong but realistic extension of a multi-field purchase flow, especially for gift-card purchases involving recipient and delivery data. It stays tightly anchored to the accepted configuration requirement while likely probing a missing visible phase.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall allow a shopper to configure an eGift card purchase by entering recipient details, sender details, denomination, delivery date, and a personal message before purchase.

requirements.text: The system shall display a synchronized purchase summary on the eGift card purchase page that visibly updates the currently selected amount and entered delivery details as the shopper configures the card.
requirements.rationale:
Contrastive import from staged bundle
Intended label: not_fulfilled
Mutation family: stronger_visible_constraint
Source gold requirement: REQ-09
Source gold text: The system shall provide an action for the configured eGift card on the product-detail page to get to the shopping cart.
Generation rationale: The accepted flow already supports configuration on the product page and progression toward the bag. Requiring a live synchronized summary is a disciplined strengthening of visible purchase transparency that remains close to the same page-level workflow but is likely unsupported in the shown flow.
requirements.parent_harvest_text: The system shall provide an action for the configured eGift card on the product-detail page to get to the shopping cart.
